description: A vertical, candid color photograph (no on-image text or graphic design) of a Pembroke Welsh Corgi standing in a field of orange and yellow wildflowers (California poppies and similar blooms) at golden hour. The dog is tan/red and white, tongue out, smiling toward the camera, wearing a harness with a black leash extending to the upper right. Background shows a utility pole, scattered bare trees, distant houses and a soft sunset sky. The shot is a glossy, well-composed but ordinary smartphone-style pet portrait, not a designed graphic. It accompanies a light, morale/'humanizing the agency' social-media post introducing 'Murphy von Peanut,' the corgi of a DOL Office of the Solicitor litigator named Rose. The intent is soft-power public engagement -- making the Department of Labor feel relatable and friendly rather than delivering policy messaging. No agency seal, slogan, or designed typography appears on the image itself.
notable_text: (none on image; tweet text only -- introduces 'Murphy von Peanut, corgi extraordinaire')
